description: Sepsis and shock cases for Resident Life simulator - Sepsis-3 criteria, fluid resuscitation, vasopressors, source control

patients.name: Mr. Rashid
patients.complaint: Fever 38.9°C, rigors, confusion for 1 day. Urinary frequency and dysuria for 3 days. Indwelling Foley catheter for 2 weeks post-TURP. Hypotensive: BP 82/48, HR 118, RR 32.
patients.triageNote: 72M, febrile, confused, hypotensive, catheterized, ?urosepsis, HR 118, BP 82/48, SpO2 94%, RR 32
patients.correctDiagnosis: Septic Shock secondary to Catheter-Associated Urinary Tract Infection (CAUTI)

result.finding: Lactate: 5.8 mmol/L
result.interpretation: Markedly elevated (>4.0) = tissue hypoperfusion. Meets Sepsis-3 septic shock criteria: vasopressors needed to maintain MAP ≥65 + lactate >2 after fluids.

result.finding: Cultures drawn. Preliminary: Gram-negative rods in blood and urine at 24h. Final: Escherichia coli, ESBL-negative, sensitive to ceftriaxone.
result.interpretation: E. coli = most common CAUTI organism. Gram-negative bacteremia = endotoxin-mediated shock.

result.finding: WBC 19.8, Neutrophils 91%, Bands 15%, Plt 85, Hb 13.5
result.interpretation: Leukocytosis with severe left shift (bands 15%). Thrombocytopenia (Plt 85) suggests early DIC or sepsis-induced bone marrow suppression.

result.finding: BUN 45, Creatinine 2.8 (baseline 1.0 two weeks ago). GFR 22 mL/min.
result.interpretation: AKI Stage 3 (Cr 2.8x baseline). Sepsis-associated AKI. BUN:Cr ratio >20:1 suggests pre-renal component from hypotension.

possibleActions.name: Coagulation Profile (PT, PTT, INR, Fibrinogen, D-Dimer)

result.finding: PT 18, PTT 45, INR 1.6, Fibrinogen 180, D-Dimer >20,000
result.interpretation: Coagulopathy: elevated PT/PTT, low fibrinogen, extremely high D-Dimer = DIC (Disseminated Intravascular Coagulation) from sepsis.

result.finding: Procalcitonin: 35.2 ng/mL
result.interpretation: PCT >10 = very high probability of severe bacterial sepsis. PCT >2.0 with clinical signs = bacterial infection confirmed.

result.finding: pH 7.18, pCO2 28, pO2 82, HCO3 10, Lactate 5.8, SaO2 94%
result.interpretation: Severe metabolic acidosis (high anion gap: Na - Cl - HCO3 = 28). Compensatory respiratory alkalosis (pCO2 28 = Winter's formula: expected pCO2 = 1.5(10)+8±2 = 21-25). Lactate 5.8 confirms lactic acidosis from septic shock.

result.finding: 2.5L Lactated Ringer's infused over 60 min. MAP improved from 52 to 58. Still hypotensive (MAP <65). Lactate repeat: 4.2 (decreased from 5.8).
result.interpretation: Partial response to fluids. MAP still <65 = requires vasopressors. Lactate clearance 27% (good response, target >20%). Continue fluids cautiously.

result.finding: Norepinephrine started at 0.1 mcg/kg/min titrated to MAP ≥65. MAP now 68. Still on moderate dose.
result.interpretation: Norepinephrine = first-line vasopressor for septic shock (Surviving Sepsis Campaign). Target MAP ≥65. If dose >0.3 mcg/kg/min, add vasopressin.

result.finding: Piperacillin-Tazobactam 4.5g IV started within 45 minutes of arrival. Covering gram-negative rods (including Pseudomonas) and anaerobes.
result.interpretation: Empiric therapy for urosepsis with AKI: Pip-Tazo covers E. coli, Klebsiella, Proteus, Pseudomonas. Avoid aminoglycosides in renal failure.

result.finding: Foley catheter removed. New catheter placed after urine culture obtained. Source of infection addressed.
result.interpretation: Source control is CRITICAL in sepsis management. Without removing infected catheter, antibiotics alone insufficient.

treatmentPathway.wrongActionsConsequence: Delay in any of: antibiotics, fluids, vasopressors, or source control increases mortality significantly

criticalErrors.consequence: Every hour delay in antibiotics in septic shock increases mortality by 7-8%. SEP-1 requires antibiotics within 1 hour.
Without removing infected catheter, antibiotics cannot clear infection. Persistent bacteremia = ongoing shock.

stages.event: Worsening shock. MAP 45. Lactate 8.2. Anuric.
Multi-organ failure: AKI, DIC, ARDS. pH 7.02.
Refractory shock despite maximum vasopressors.
Cardiac arrest. Death.
MAP 60 with fluids alone. Still needs vasopressors.
Lactate 3.8. Improving but shock not resolved.
Without antibiotics and source control, infection progressing.
MAP 68 on norepinephrine 0.15 mcg/kg/min. Lactate 3.2 (down from 5.8).
Urine output improving. Lactate 2.1. Reducing vasopressor dose.
MAP 72 on norepinephrine 0.05. Lactate 1.4. Shock resolving.
Off vasopressors. Lactate 1.1. AKI improving. ICU transfer for monitoring.

learningPoints.missedDiagnosis: If you thought it was cardiogenic shock: Warm extremities + bounding pulses + fever = septic shock, not cardiogenic. Cardiogenic shock has cold clammy extremities + pulmonary edema + JVD.
learningPoints.keyTeaching: Sepsis-3 Definition: SOFA score increase ≥2 + infection. Septic shock: Vasopressors to maintain MAP ≥65 + Lactate >2 mmol/L after adequate fluid resuscitation. Surviving Sepsis Hour-1 Bundle: Measure lactate, Blood cultures, Broad-spectrum antibiotics, Crystalloid 30mL/kg, Vasopressors if hypotensive during/after fluids.
learningPoints.guideline: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021: Hour-1 Bundle (all within 60 minutes): 1) Measure lactate, 2) Blood cultures before antibiotics, 3) Broad-spectrum antibiotics, 4) 30mL/kg crystalloid for hypotension/lactate ≥4, 5) Vasopressors if hypotensive during/after fluid resuscitation.

patients.name: Mrs. Huda
patients.complaint: Fever 38.5°C, chills, right upper quadrant pain for 2 days. Jaundiced. Confused today. BP 88/52, HR 122, RR 28. Diabetes, previous cholelithiasis.
patients.triageNote: 58F, febrile, jaundiced, RUQ pain, confused, hypotensive, ?cholangitis, HR 122, BP 88/52
patients.correctDiagnosis: Septic Shock secondary to Acute Cholangitis (Charcot's Triad + Reynolds' Pentad)

result.finding: Lactate: 6.2 mmol/L
result.interpretation: Severe hyperlactatemia = tissue hypoperfusion. Septic shock criteria met.

result.finding: Total Bilirubin 8.5, Direct 6.2, ALP 420, GGT 580, ALT 180, AST 150, Albumin 2.8
result.interpretation: Cholestatic pattern (ALP/GGT >> ALT/AST). Direct hyperbilirubinemia = biliary obstruction. Cholangitis pattern.

result.finding: WBC 24.5, Neutrophils 94%, Bands 18%, Plt 65, Hb 13.1
result.interpretation: Severe leukocytosis with left shift (bands 18%). Thrombocytopenia (Plt 65) suggests sepsis-induced or early DIC.

result.finding: Preliminary at 24h: Gram-negative rods. Final: Escherichia coli + Klebsiella pneumoniae (ESBL-negative).
result.interpretation: Polymicrobial gram-negative bacteremia from biliary source

result.finding: Gallstones in gallbladder. Dilated common bile duct 12mm (normal <6mm). CBD stone visualized in distal duct. Gallbladder wall thickened 6mm. No pericholecystic fluid.
result.interpretation: Choledocholithiasis with biliary obstruction causing cholangitis. CBD dilation + stone + clinical Charcot's triad = acute cholangitis.

result.finding: Dilated CBD 12mm with obstructing stone. Pneumobilia. Gallbladder distended. No abscess. No pancreatitis.
result.interpretation: Confirms choledocholithiasis with cholangitis. Pneumobilia suggests gas-forming organism or previous instrumentation.

result.finding: 2L LR infused. MAP improved from 50 to 56. Still hypotensive (MAP <65). Lactate repeat: 4.8 (improved from 6.2).
result.interpretation: Partial response. Vasopressors needed.

result.finding: Norepinephrine 0.2 mcg/kg/min. MAP 67. Target MAP ≥65 achieved.
result.interpretation: Vasopressor support required = septic shock (Sepsis-3).

result.finding: Piperacillin-Tazobactam 4.5g IV started within 40 min. Covering gram-negative rods + anaerobes.
result.interpretation: Cholangitis empiric therapy: Must cover Enterobacteriaceae (E. coli, Klebsiella) + anaerobes. Pip-Tazo or Ceftriaxone + Metronidazole.

possibleActions.name: Emergency ERCP with Stone Extraction + Biliary Drainage

result.finding: ERCP performed. CBD stone extracted. Bile drained (purulent). CBD stent placed. Patient improved dramatically within hours.
result.interpretation: ERCP = definitive source control for cholangitis. Mortality without biliary drainage: 50-100%. With ERCP: <10%.

treatmentPathway.wrongActionsConsequence: Without ERCP/biliary drainage, antibiotics alone cannot resolve cholangitis. Mortality approaches 100% without source control.

criticalErrors.consequence: ERCP is URGENT (within 24h for severe cholangitis, within 6-12h for septic shock). Mortality increases with delay. Antibiotics alone insufficient - source control required.

stages.event: Worsening shock. MAP 42. Anuric.
Multi-organ failure. pH 7.05. Lactate 12.
Death from septic shock.
Antibiotics helping but obstruction not relieved. Ongoing bacteremia.
Liver abscess forming. Shock worsening.
Death without biliary drainage.
Post-ERCP. Bile draining. Shock improving.
MAP 75 off vasopressors. Lactate 1.8. Fever resolving.
Stable. Transfer to ward. Plan for elective cholecystectomy in 6 weeks.

learningPoints.missedDiagnosis: If you thought it was cholecystitis without cholangitis: Charcot's triad (fever + RUQ pain + jaundice) = cholangitis. Reynolds' pentad (Charcot's + hypotension + confusion) = severe cholangitis with septic shock. This is a GI emergency requiring urgent biliary drainage.
learningPoints.keyTeaching: Cholangitis severity: Grade I (mild) = supportive care + antibiotics. Grade II (moderate) = early biliary drainage (within 24h). Grade III (severe) = urgent biliary drainage (within 6-12h) + organ support. ERCP is gold standard. PTBD if ERCP fails.
learningPoints.guideline: Tokyo Guidelines 2018: Definitive diagnosis of cholangitis = Charcot's triad + lab evidence of inflammation + biliary obstruction on imaging. Severe cholangitis (Grade III) = organ dysfunction (hypotension, altered mental status, PaO2/FiO2 <300, AKI, liver dysfunction, thrombocytopenia).

conceptsTested: Sepsis-3 criteria and septic shock recognition
Cholangitis: Charcot's triad, Reynolds' pentad, Tokyo Guidelines
